4.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very

7.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

7.gl: persuasion, i.e. credence; moral conviction (of religious truth, or the truthfulness of God or a religious teacher), especially reliance upon Christ for salvation; abstractly, constancy in such profession; by extension,

something said (including the thought); by implication, a topic (subject of discourse), also reasoning (the mental faculty) or motive; by extension, a computation; specially, (with the article in John) the Divine Expres

9.gl: graciousness (as gratifying), of manner or act (abstract or concrete; literal, figurative or spiritual; especially the divine influence upon the heart, and its reflection in the life; including gratitude)
9.kj: acceptable, benefit, favour, gift, grace(- ious), joy, liberality, pleasure, thank(-s, -worthy)

9.gl: the heart, i.e. (figuratively) the thoughts or feelings (mind); also (by analogy) the middle
